dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 425 RH: Repealing Big Brother Overreach Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Davidson (for himself, Mr. Balderson, Mr. Bergman, Mr. Biggs of Arizona, Mr. Buchanan, Mr. Burlison, Mrs. Cammack, Mr. Ciscomani, Mr. Cloud, Mr. Clyde, Mr. Crane, Mr. Dunn of Florida, Mr. Edwards, Mr. Ellzey, Mr. Estes, Mr. Ezell, Mr. Finstad, Mrs. Fischbach, Mr. Fleischmann, Ms. Foxx, Mr. Fulcher, Mr. Graves, Ms. Greene of Georgia, Mr. Grothman, Mr. Guest, Ms. Hageman, Mr. Hern of Oklahoma, Mrs. Houchin, Mr. Hudson, Mr. Johnson of South Dakota, Mr. LaHood, Mr. Langworthy, Ms. Lee of Florida, Ms. Mace, Mr. Moolenaar, Mr. Moore of Alabama, Mr. Norman, Mr. Ogles, Mr. Perry, Mr. Pfluger, Mr. Rose, Mr. Rouzer, Mr. Roy, Mr. Rulli, Mr. Schweikert, Mr. Smith of Missouri, Mr. Smith of Nebraska, Mr. Stauber, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania, Mr. Tiffany, Ms. Van Duyne, Mr. Weber of Texas, Mr. Zinke, Mr. Collins, Mr. Bost, Mr. Kelly of Mississippi, Mr. Scott Franklin of Florida, Mr. Bean of Florida, Mr. Stutzman, Mr. Taylor, Mr. Barrett, Mr. Downing, Mr. Gill of Texas, Mr. Yakym, Mr. Van Orden, Mrs. Bice, Mr. Onder, and Mr. Griffith) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

Additional sponsors: Mr. Murphy, Mrs. Harshbarger, Mr. Allen, Mr. Begich, Mr. Schmidt, Mr. Cole, Mr. Self, Mr. Obernolte, Mr. Crank, Mr. Fallon, Mr. Fry, Mr. Comer, Mr. Palmer, Mr. Bacon, Mr. Gosar, Mr. Nehls, Mr. Webster of Florida, Mr. Kennedy of Utah, Mr. McGuire, Mr. Bilirakis, Mr. Steube, Mrs. Miller-Meeks, Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia, Mr. Strong, Mrs. Miller of West Virginia, Mr. Walberg, Mr. Nunn of Iowa, Mr. Green of Tennessee, Mr. Higgins of Louisiana, Mrs. Hinson, Mr. Goldman of Texas, Mr. Westerman, Mr. Miller of Ohio, Mr. DesJarlais, Mr. Gooden, Mr. Moore of West Virginia, Mr. Newhouse, Mr. Moran, Mr. Moore of North Carolina, Mr. Carter of Texas, Mr. Carey, Mr. Evans of Colorado, Mr. Feenstra, Mr. Donalds, Mr. Shreve, Mr. Mills, Mr. Wied, Ms. Malliotakis, Mr. Latta, Mr. Kelly of Pennsylvania, Mr. Carter of Georgia, Mr. Valadao, Mr. Hunt, Mr. Smucker, Mr. Flood, Mr. Timmons, Mrs. Fedorchak, Mr. Brecheen, Mr. Jackson of Texas, Mr. Alford, Mr. Turner of Ohio, Mr. Amodei of Nevada, Mr. Baird, Mr. Hurd of Colorado, Mr. Sessions, Mr. LaMalfa, Mr. Messmer, Mr. McCormick, Mr. McDowell, Mr. Burchett, Mr. Tony Gonzales of Texas, Mr. Hamadeh of Arizona, Ms. Maloy, Mrs. Miller of Illinois, Mr. Harris of Maryland, Mr. Bresnahan, Mr. Jordan, Mr. Patronis, Mr. Harris of North Carolina, Mr. Owens, Ms. Boebert, Mr. Jack, Mr. Kustoff, Mr. Simpson, Mr. Huizenga, Mr. Arrington, Mr. Mackenzie, Mr. Wittman, Mr. Lawler, Mr. Barr, Mr. Rogers of Kentucky, Mr. Fine, Mr. Joyce of Ohio, Mr. Joyce of Pennsylvania, Mr. Mann, Ms. Letlow, Mr. Rogers of Alabama, Mr. Guthrie, Mr. Fong, Mr. Babin, Mr. Aderholt, Mrs. Biggs of South Carolina, Mr. Cline, Mr. McCaul, Mr. LaLota, Mr. Van Drew, Mr. Moore of Utah, Ms. Salazar, Mrs. Luna, Ms. Stefanik, Mr. Wilson of South Carolina, Mr. Bentz, Mr. Massie, Mr. Calvert, Mr. Kean, Mr. Baumgartner, Mr. Austin Scott of Georgia, Mr. Issa, Mr. Meuser, Mr. Rutherford, Mr. Mast, Ms. De La Cruz, Mr. Gimenez, Mr. Harrigan, and Mr. Knott
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on January 15, 2025

form.official-title: To repeal the Corporate Transparency Act.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Repealing Big Brother Overreach Act.

subsection.text: Section 5336 of title 31, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting foreign after Beneficial each place such term appears in heading or text;

by inserting foreign after beneficial each place such term appears in heading or text (and conforming the item relating to such section 5336 in the table of contents for chapter 53 of title 31, United States Code, accordingly); and

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3)(A), by inserting after individual who the following: is a foreign person and; and
in paragraph (11)(A), by striking that is and all that follows through (ii) formed and inserting that is formed.

subsection.header: Deletion of data relating to non-reporting companies
subsection.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network shall delete all beneficial ownership information collected pursuant to section 5336 of title 31, United States Code, with respect to—

paragraph.text: any individual who is not a foreign beneficial owner (as such term is defined under such section 5336); or
a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity that is not a reporting company (as such term is defined under such section 5336).

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
